metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_296
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Increased endocytosis in the absence of Calnexin in cultured cells (mouse neuroblastoma) and neurons. Significantly increased numbers of synaptic vesicles in cerebellar synapses (Fig. 2e-f).
Formally the authors demonstrate in non-neuronal cells that Calnexin is an involved in regulating endocytosis by inhibiting transferrin uptake (Fig. 3). From the KO phenotype one can conclude that endocytosis of synaptic vesicles is also affected.

14/3/2017 Pim
- Literal: "Here we showed that the calnexin C-tail is a potent inhibitor of endocytosis in neuronal cells indicating a functional specialization of calnexin domains. "
- EM: "For electron microscopy analysis, 7-day old mice were euthanized by decapitation and their cerebella were taken.". Experimental description: Cerebellum was isolated from a 7-day-old mouse, cut into small pieces and digested in trypsin for 12 min at 37°C. p.

- EM: "For electron microscopy analysis, 7-day old mice were euthanized by decapitation and their cerebella were taken."
